question: A project manager is leading a product launch for a retail client. The team has completed 60% of the planned features, and the client now wants to go live with what is ready to capture the holiday shopping season. The remaining features are rated low priority. What should the project manager do?
options: Refuse the request and insist all features be completed before release to maintain quality standards.
Negotiate with the client to release the completed high-priority features now and defer low-priority features to a later phase.
Compress the schedule by adding overtime so all features can be included in the original launch.
Escalate the situation to the sponsor and wait for a directive before proceeding.
explanation: PMI emphasizes delivering business value urgently. Releasing completed high-priority features now captures the business opportunity (holiday sales) while deferring low-value work aligns with the MVP mindset and prevents gold-plating. Forcing all features delays value delivery unnecessarily.
explanation_vi: PMI nhấn mạnh việc phân phối giá trị kinh doanh khẩn cấp. Phát hành các tính năng ưu tiên cao đã hoàn thành ngay bây giờ sẽ nắm bắt được cơ hội kinh doanh (doanh số mùa lễ) trong khi việc hoãn công việc ít giá trị phù hợp với tư duy MVP và ngăn chặn gold-plating.

question: During sprint planning, the product owner presents 12 user stories in the backlog. The team's velocity allows completing only 6 stories per sprint. The product owner insists all 12 stories must be done in this sprint for a big-bang release. What is the BEST approach?
options: Agree to take all 12 stories and plan to work overtime to meet the product owner's expectation.
Work with the product owner to identify the 6 highest-value stories that deliver a shippable increment, and defer the rest.
Split the team into two groups to attempt parallel development of all 12 stories.
Reject the sprint and request a new backlog refinement session before committing to any work.
explanation: Agile teams commit to sustainable pace and deliver shippable increments each sprint. Working with the product owner to prioritize the 6 highest-value stories ensures the team delivers business value urgently without overcommitting. Big-bang releases delay value delivery and increase risk.
explanation_vi: Các nhóm Agile cam kết nhịp độ bền vững và phân phối các gia tăng có thể bàn giao mỗi sprint. Làm việc với product owner để ưu tiên 6 user story có giá trị cao nhất đảm bảo nhóm phân phối giá trị kinh doanh khẩn cấp mà không cam kết quá mức.

question: A project manager notices the team has spent two weeks perfecting the UI styling on a feature that was already accepted by the stakeholder. The feature meets all acceptance criteria but the team continues making minor cosmetic improvements. What should the project manager do?
options: Allow the team to continue since improved quality always benefits the customer.
Ask the team lead to document the extra work as scope changes and submit for approval.
Redirect the team to stop unsanctioned enhancements and focus on delivering the next highest-priority item in the backlog.
Schedule a design review meeting with stakeholders to decide if the cosmetic improvements are worth keeping.
explanation: This is a classic gold-plating scenario. The feature already meets acceptance criteria, so additional unsanctioned work consumes time and budget without delivering business value. The project manager must redirect the team to the next highest-priority work to maximize value delivery urgency.
explanation_vi: Đây là tình huống gold-plating điển hình. Tính năng đã đáp ứng tiêu chí chấp nhận, vì vậy công việc bổ sung không được phép tiêu tốn thời gian và ngân sách mà không mang lại giá trị kinh doanh. PM phải chuyển hướng nhóm sang công việc ưu tiên cao nhất tiếp theo.

question: A hybrid project team is behind schedule. The project manager reviews the work breakdown structure and identifies that three low-priority deliverables account for 30% of remaining effort. These deliverables were added based on a stakeholder's 'nice-to-have' request early in the project. What is the MOST appropriate action?
options: Continue executing all deliverables as planned to respect the original scope baseline.
Facilitate a value vs. effort analysis with the stakeholder to formally descope or defer the low-priority deliverables.
Crash the schedule by adding resources to complete all deliverables on time.
Complete the low-priority deliverables first to clear them from the backlog before tackling critical work.
explanation: When behind schedule, eliminating low-value work through formal descoping is the lean approach. Facilitating a value vs. effort conversation with the stakeholder respects governance while focusing the team on highest-value delivery. Crashing adds cost, and completing low-priority work first worsens the delay.
explanation_vi: Khi chậm tiến độ, việc loại bỏ công việc ít giá trị thông qua loại bỏ phạm vi chính thức là cách tiếp cận tinh gọn. Tạo điều kiện cho cuộc trò chuyện về giá trị so với nỗ lực với stakeholder tôn trọng quản trị trong khi tập trung nhóm vào việc phân phối giá trị cao nhất.

question: A scrum team completes a potentially shippable increment at the end of each sprint but the product owner consistently says 'let's wait until we have more features before releasing.' After four sprints, a competitor releases a similar product. What PMI principle was most violated?
options: Plan thoroughly before executing to avoid rework.
Deliver value incrementally and frequently to realize benefits early and reduce risk.
Ensure full stakeholder alignment before any release.
Maintain a fixed scope to protect the project baseline.
explanation: PMI's value delivery principle states that teams should deliver value incrementally and frequently. Waiting for a 'big release' defers business value realization, increases risk, and as shown here, can result in losing competitive advantage. Each potentially shippable increment should be evaluated for immediate release.
explanation_vi: Nguyên tắc phân phối giá trị của PMI nêu rằng các nhóm nên phân phối giá trị gia tăng và thường xuyên. Chờ đợi 'phát hành lớn' trì hoãn việc nhận ra giá trị kinh doanh, tăng rủi ro và như đã thấy ở đây, có thể dẫn đến mất lợi thế cạnh tranh.

question: A project manager is executing a software implementation project. The business sponsor mentions they need a specific report capability within 3 weeks to support a board presentation. This requirement is not in the current scope. What should the project manager do FIRST?
options: Immediately assign a developer to build the report to satisfy the sponsor's urgent need.
Assess the impact of this request on current deliverables, then engage the sponsor in a prioritization discussion about trade-offs.
Deny the request since it was not included in the original scope and would set a precedent for uncontrolled changes.
Add the requirement to the backlog and address it after the current phase is complete.
explanation: Before acting, the project manager must assess impact. This protects the project while acknowledging the urgency. A transparent trade-off conversation lets the sponsor make an informed decision — perhaps descoping other work or approving additional resources — ensuring the team focuses on highest-value outcomes.
explanation_vi: Trước khi hành động, PM phải đánh giá tác động. Điều này bảo vệ dự án trong khi thừa nhận tính khẩn cấp. Một cuộc trò chuyện đánh đổi minh bạch cho phép nhà tài trợ đưa ra quyết định sáng suốt - có thể giảm phạm vi công việc khác hoặc phê duyệt thêm tài nguyên.

question: An agile team is planning their next sprint. The product owner wants to maximize business value delivery. Which TWO actions BEST reflect PMI's value delivery and urgency principles?
options: Select user stories strictly in priority order from the top of the backlog based on business value.
Include technical debt stories interchangeably with feature stories to balance the sprint.
Ensure each selected story can be fully completed and demonstrated by end of sprint.
Add a buffer of lower-priority stories in case the team finishes early.
explanation: Selecting highest-priority stories (A) ensures the team works on what delivers the most business value first. Ensuring each story can be fully completed (C) supports delivering a shippable increment — both are core to PMI's urgency and incremental value delivery principles. Adding low-priority buffer stories (D) wastes capacity that could be saved for emerging high-value work.
explanation_vi: Chọn các story ưu tiên cao nhất (A) đảm bảo nhóm làm việc trên những gì mang lại giá trị kinh doanh lớn nhất trước tiên. Đảm bảo mỗi story có thể được hoàn thành đầy đủ (C) hỗ trợ việc phân phối gia tăng có thể bàn giao - cả hai đều là cốt lõi của các nguyên tắc phân phối giá trị khẩn cấp và gia tăng của PMI.

question: A project is 70% complete. A key stakeholder requests adding 15 new features, arguing it will make the product more comprehensive. The project manager knows these features would extend the timeline by 4 months and delay benefits realization. What is the BEST response?
options: Accept all 15 features since the stakeholder is a key decision-maker whose satisfaction is paramount.
Accept only the features that the project manager personally deems important.
Present the cost-benefit analysis showing delayed value realization and facilitate a prioritization session to identify only the features that justify the delay.
Reject all 15 features citing scope freeze and escalate to the project sponsor.
explanation: PMI's value delivery approach requires that scope changes be evaluated against their business benefit. Presenting a cost-benefit analysis quantifies the trade-off (4-month delay in benefits realization) and empowers the stakeholder to make an informed decision. The goal is to maximize business value, not to simply accept or reject all requests.
explanation_vi: Cách tiếp cận phân phối giá trị của PMI yêu cầu các thay đổi phạm vi phải được đánh giá dựa trên lợi ích kinh doanh của chúng. Trình bày phân tích chi phí-lợi ích định lượng sự đánh đổi và trao quyền cho stakeholder đưa ra quyết định sáng suốt.

question: A hybrid project team uses two-week iterations. Midway through iteration 3, the business analyst discovers a regulatory requirement that, if missed, would make the entire product unusable. This requirement was not in the original scope. What should the project manager do?
options: Log it in the risk register and address it after the current phase ends to avoid disrupting the iteration.
Stop all current work immediately and redirect the entire team to the regulatory requirement.
Immediately escalate to the sponsor, assess impact, and re-prioritize the backlog to address the regulatory item with urgency in the next iteration at the latest.
Submit a change request through the formal change control board and wait for approval before taking action.
explanation: Regulatory non-compliance eliminates all business value from the product — this is a critical, urgent issue. PMI's principle of delivering value with urgency means some situations demand immediate reprioritization. The project manager escalates quickly, assesses impact, and ensures the team addresses this before it jeopardizes the entire project outcome.
explanation_vi: Không tuân thủ quy định loại bỏ tất cả giá trị kinh doanh khỏi sản phẩm - đây là vấn đề quan trọng và khẩn cấp. Nguyên tắc của PMI về phân phối giá trị với tính khẩn cấp có nghĩa là một số tình huống đòi hỏi phải tái ưu tiên ngay lập tức.

question: During project execution, the project manager tracks that the team spends 40% of their time in status meetings, documentation updates, and internal reporting. These activities produce no direct deliverable value. What should the project manager do to improve value delivery?
options: Accept this overhead as a normal and necessary part of project management.
Conduct a process audit to identify and eliminate or streamline non-value-adding activities, freeing the team to focus on deliverables.
Add more team members to absorb the overhead so delivery work is not impacted.
Ask team members to work longer hours to compensate for time lost to administrative tasks.
explanation: PMI's lean delivery approach treats non-value-adding activities as waste. A process audit to identify and eliminate unnecessary overhead directly increases the team's productive capacity and accelerates value delivery. Adding people or extending hours are anti-patterns that add cost without addressing the root cause.
explanation_vi: Cách tiếp cận phân phối tinh gọn của PMI coi các hoạt động không tạo ra giá trị là lãng phí. Kiểm toán quy trình để xác định và loại bỏ chi phí không cần thiết trực tiếp tăng công suất sản xuất của nhóm và tăng tốc độ phân phối giá trị.

question: A project is in execution. The earned value analysis shows SPI = 0.75 and CPI = 1.10. The project manager must decide how to recover the schedule. Which action BEST aligns with PMI's value delivery principles?
options: Crash all remaining activities by adding resources across the board to recover schedule.
Fast-track the entire remaining work by running all activities in parallel regardless of dependencies.
Identify which remaining activities on the critical path deliver the highest business value and focus recovery efforts there first.
Extend the project deadline to allow the team to complete all work at the current pace without pressure.
explanation: PMI's value-focused approach means schedule recovery should be targeted at activities that matter most to business outcomes. Crashing or fast-tracking everything wastes resources on low-value work. Focusing recovery on high-value critical path activities delivers the most important outcomes faster, aligning with urgency principles.
explanation_vi: Cách tiếp cận tập trung vào giá trị của PMI có nghĩa là việc phục hồi tiến độ nên được nhắm mục tiêu vào các hoạt động quan trọng nhất đối với kết quả kinh doanh. Tập trung phục hồi vào các hoạt động đường găng có giá trị cao phân phối các kết quả quan trọng nhất nhanh hơn.

question: An agile project manager wants to ensure the team consistently delivers business value with urgency. Which TWO metrics BEST measure this outcome according to PMI principles?
options: Number of story points completed per sprint.
Cycle time from idea to working feature delivered to users.
Percentage of planned work completed on time.
Customer satisfaction scores and realized business outcomes per release.
explanation: PMI measures success by business outcomes, not just activity. Cycle time (B) measures delivery speed — how quickly value flows from idea to customer. Customer satisfaction and business outcomes (D) measure whether delivered work actually achieved its purpose. Story points (A) and on-time completion (C) measure team activity, not business value delivered.
explanation_vi: PMI đo lường thành công bằng kết quả kinh doanh, không chỉ hoạt động. Thời gian chu kỳ (B) đo tốc độ phân phối. Sự hài lòng của khách hàng và kết quả kinh doanh (D) đo liệu công việc đã thực sự đạt được mục đích hay không.

question: A project manager is overseeing a 12-month product development project. Six months in, 80% of the highest-priority features are complete and could be released. The remaining 20% are lower-priority enhancements. The business development team says an early release would generate revenue 3 months ahead of plan. What should the project manager recommend?
options: Continue with the original plan to deliver a complete, polished product at month 12.
Recommend an early release of the 80% complete product now, with the remaining enhancements in a subsequent release.
Wait until 90% completion to ensure quality before recommending any early release.
Let the project sponsor decide without providing a recommendation since this is a business decision outside the project manager's scope.
explanation: PMI's business value urgency principle supports early release when the product can deliver meaningful value. Releasing 80% of features (the highest-priority ones) 3 months early generates tangible revenue and business benefit. The project manager's role includes advising on when to deliver value — deferring to the sponsor without analysis abdicates this responsibility.
explanation_vi: Nguyên tắc khẩn cấp giá trị kinh doanh của PMI hỗ trợ phát hành sớm khi sản phẩm có thể cung cấp giá trị có ý nghĩa. Phát hành 80% tính năng sớm hơn 3 tháng tạo ra doanh thu và lợi ích kinh doanh hữu hình.

question: A senior developer on the project recommends building a highly flexible, configurable architecture that will support all possible future requirements, even though none of these future requirements have been confirmed. This would add 6 weeks to the schedule. What should the project manager do?
options: Approve the recommendation since technical excellence always justifies additional investment.
Ask the developer to document the idea for future consideration, but proceed with the architecture needed to meet confirmed current requirements.
Escalate to the technical architect and defer the decision.
Accept half of the developer's suggestion as a compromise to maintain team morale.
explanation: Building for unconfirmed future requirements is gold-plating and over-engineering — forms of waste that delay value delivery without confirmed benefit. PMI's MVP mindset directs teams to build what is needed now. The developer's idea has merit for a future phase but should not delay current value delivery based on speculation.
explanation_vi: Xây dựng cho các yêu cầu tương lai chưa được xác nhận là gold-plating và kỹ thuật quá mức - các hình thức lãng phí làm chậm trễ việc phân phối giá trị mà không có lợi ích được xác nhận. Tư duy MVP của PMI hướng dẫn các nhóm xây dựng những gì cần thiết ngay bây giờ.

question: A project manager is presenting execution progress to the steering committee. The committee is focused on the percentage of activities completed. The project manager believes a different view is more meaningful. What reporting approach BEST reflects PMI's value delivery principles?
options: Report activities completed and hours burned as these objectively measure team effort and efficiency.
Report only schedule variance and cost variance metrics from earned value analysis.
Report the percentage of planned business outcomes and benefits realized versus what was expected at this stage, alongside delivery progress.
Report team velocity and burn-down charts to show agile delivery performance.
explanation: PMI's principles focus on measuring business outcomes, not just task completion. Reporting benefits realized versus expected directly answers whether the project is delivering value — the steering committee's primary concern. Activity counts and schedule metrics tell what the team has done; outcomes reporting tells whether it matters.
explanation_vi: Các nguyên tắc của PMI tập trung vào việc đo lường kết quả kinh doanh, không chỉ hoàn thành nhiệm vụ. Báo cáo lợi ích thực hiện so với dự kiến trực tiếp trả lời liệu dự án có đang phân phối giá trị hay không - mối quan tâm chính của ủy ban chỉ đạo.
